draftNote: Promoted from draft to owner-approved design authority by CBW-SITE-TEMPLATE-ARCHITECTURE-AUTHORITY-001-COMMIT.
ownerDecisions: Exchange-review template v2 approved as the authority reference for the exchange-review family.
First Screen Conversion Standard v1 established project-wide for eligible, evidence-verified offers.
Ten page families and the rollout order approved as the site template architecture.
Never display a global promotion as locally eligible without country-level evidence.
urlPatternNote: Patterns marked proposed require separate owner routing approval; live patterns recorded as-is.

pageFamilies.name: Homepage
pageFamilies.purpose: Global or country-resolved discovery of the best eligible exchanges and promo codes.
pageFamilies.userIntent: Which exchange should I use and what code do I enter?
pageFamilies.primaryDataSource: Owner-approved offer registry + canonical Market Intelligence states (country-resolved when available).

desktopAimsToShow: status, promo code, Copy and registration CTA for each authorized row
mobileAimsToShow: at least the first two compact eligible exchange rows
complete promo code, Copy and CTA for each visible authorized row

pageFamilies.firstScreenConversionRequirement: First Screen Standard per authorized row; no oversized cards preventing commercial rows from entering the first screen.
pageFamilies.bottomConversionBehavior: Optional compact closing action (directory/finder); no forced full offer block.
pageFamilies.availabilityStateBehavior: Restricted exchanges never appear as claimable recommendations; fewer verified eligible exchanges mean fewer rows; never fill the list with unverified offers.
pageFamilies.mobileBehavior: First two eligible rows within first-screen target; 44px+ touch targets; no horizontal overflow.
prohibitedBehavior: oversized hero cards displacing commercial rows
global promos shown as locally eligible without evidence

pageFamilies.name: Country page
pageFamilies.purpose: Country-specific exchange availability, ranking, restrictions and local payment context.
pageFamilies.userIntent: Which exchanges work in my country, and which codes are valid here?
pageFamilies.primaryDataSource: Canonical Market Intelligence country cells + owner-approved country evidence; never staging or RECOVERED/UNVERIFIED packages.

firstScreenStructure.desktopTarget: Top 3 eligible rows with complete permitted conversion controls
firstScreenStructure.mobileTarget: first two eligible rows with complete permitted conversion controls

pageFamilies.firstScreenConversionRequirement: First Screen Standard per eligible row; country verdict precedes conversion rows.
mainBodySections: Available but not currently recommended (separate compact section)
pageFamilies.bottomConversionBehavior: Compact offer of the leading eligible exchange, or alternatives module when none eligible.
pageFamilies.availabilityStateBehavior: Never mix restricted exchanges into the recommended ranking; each row renders its canonical state.
pageFamilies.mobileBehavior: Verdict + first two eligible rows in first-screen target; sections collapse cleanly.
pageFamilies.seoIndexingPosture: Indexable only after country evidence and publication are owner-approved.
prohibitedBehavior: restricted exchanges in the recommended ranking

pageFamilies.name: Exchange directory
pageFamilies.userIntent: Show me the options; let me filter by what matters to me.
pageFamilies.primaryDataSource: Offer registry + canonical MI availability badges.

contains: one compact recommended or featured eligible offer row when evidence allows

pageFamilies.firstScreenConversionRequirement: Search/filter primacy; featured row follows the First Screen Standard; no forced promo offer without an authorized featured exchange.
mainBodySections: result rows with status, compact code chip and CTA per eligible row
pageFamilies.availabilityStateBehavior: Results clearly distinguish available, available with limits, restricted, under review; restricted rows carry no claim controls.
pageFamilies.mobileBehavior: Filters collapse; one row per line; codes copyable; no overflow.
prohibitedBehavior: large unrelated promo hero displacing search

pageFamilies.name: Exchange review
pageFamilies.purpose: Full exchange evaluation plus authorized conversion; approved prototype is the authority reference.
pageFamilies.userIntent: Is this exchange good, and what is the code?
pageFamilies.primaryDataSource: Offer registry + canonical MI state + dated evidence registry.
pageFamilies.firstScreenConversionRequirement: Full First Screen Standard, all state rules, all six required viewports.
pageFamilies.bottomConversionBehavior: Compact version of the same ExchangeOfferBlock; same rectangular logo, same state logic, same authorization rules.
pageFamilies.availabilityStateBehavior: AVAILABLE / AVAILABLE_WITH_LIMITS (with referralCompatibilityVerified sub-state) / RESTRICTED; both blocks always share one state.
pageFamilies.mobileBehavior: Prototype-proven 24/24 first-screen matrix; tables scroll in their own wrappers.
pageFamilies.seoIndexingPosture: Indexable exchange referral-code/review head terms; existing six live pages frozen until migration authorization.

pageFamilies.name: Exchange x Country page
pageFamilies.purpose: Explain whether a specific exchange is suitable and available in a specific country.
pageFamilies.userIntent: Does this exchange work in my country; is the code valid here?
pageFamilies.primaryDataSource: Canonical MI exchange-x-country cell with dated evidence; never staging or RECOVERED/UNVERIFIED packages.
pageFamilies.firstScreenConversionRequirement: First Screen Standard with country-scoped evidence gate; global exchange availability never overrides a country-specific restriction.
pageFamilies.bottomConversionBehavior: ExchangeOfferBlock COMPACT when eligible; alternatives module when restricted.
pageFamilies.availabilityStateBehavior: Driven solely by the canonical country cell (e.g. MEXC x KZ RESTRICTED renders restriction-only presentation).
pageFamilies.mobileBehavior: Standard viewports; local verdict + state-correct action in first screen.
pageFamilies.seoIndexingPosture: Indexable only when that country-x-exchange publication is owner-authorized.
prohibitedBehavior: global promo shown as locally eligible without evidence
overriding country restriction with global state

pageFamilies.name: Referral / promo-code page
pageFamilies.purpose: Answer code, eligibility, reward and registration questions directly.
pageFamilies.userIntent: Give me the working code now.
pageFamilies.primaryDataSource: Offer registry + canonical MI eligibility + dated offer evidence.

firstScreenStructure.rule: No large editorial introduction before the promo controls.

pageFamilies.firstScreenConversionRequirement: Code and registration CTA are the dominant first-screen action.
pageFamilies.availabilityStateBehavior: Country compatibility unverified: hide the claimable code, show explicit eligibility warning, use Check Eligibility instead of Claim Bonus. Restricted: no code or claim at all.
pageFamilies.mobileBehavior: Code + CTA fully inside the first screen at all required viewports.

pageFamilies.name: Exchange comparison page
pageFamilies.userIntent: A vs B - which should I pick and which code do I use?
pageFamilies.primaryDataSource: Canonical MI states + offer registry for each compared exchange.

firstScreenStructure.twoExchange: Both compact comparison offers in the first screen where layout allows without reducing readability; each eligible offer may show status, promo code, Copy, registration CTA.

pageFamilies.firstScreenConversionRequirement: First Screen Standard per eligible offer; readability never sacrificed for density.
pageFamilies.bottomConversionBehavior: COMPACT offer of the eligible winner only.
pageFamilies.availabilityStateBehavior: A restricted comparison winner never receives a promo code, claim CTA or registration CTA; a restricted exchange is never selected as a monetized winner.
pageFamilies.mobileBehavior: Comparison table scrolls in its own container; first two compact offers in first-screen target.

pageFamilies.name: Guide / how-to page
pageFamilies.purpose: Complete a user task such as registration, purchase, deposit, withdrawal or P2P operation.
pageFamilies.userIntent: Show me how to do this, step by step.
pageFamilies.primaryDataSource: Editorial steps + platform evidence screenshots; offer registry/MI for embedded offers.

firstScreenStructure.commercialGuides: May contain one recommended eligible exchange offer in the first screen when directly relevant to the task, with complete code and CTA when authorized.
firstScreenStructure.neutralGuides: Never forced to display a promo code.

pageFamilies.firstScreenConversionRequirement: Applies only to the embedded offer module when present.
pageFamilies.availabilityStateBehavior: Embedded modules obey full state rules; restricted exchanges get no claim language anywhere in the guide.
pageFamilies.mobileBehavior: Step 1 never displaced below the fold by a conversion element.
prohibitedBehavior: commercial module displacing the first step

pageFamilies.name: Restricted exchange / alternatives page
pageFamilies.purpose: Explain why an exchange is restricted and provide eligible alternatives.
pageFamilies.userIntent: This exchange does not work for me - why, and what should I use instead?
pageFamilies.primaryDataSource: Canonical MI restriction evidence + eligible-alternative states.

firstScreenStructure.desktopTarget: first three compact eligible alternatives where available
firstScreenStructure.mobileTarget: first two compact eligible alternatives where available

pageFamilies.firstScreenConversionRequirement: Conversion belongs to the alternatives: authorized alternatives may show promo code, Copy and registration CTA; the restricted exchange never shows those controls.
mainBodySections: what the restriction does and does not mean (no technical-inaccessibility claim unless separately verified)
pageFamilies.bottomConversionBehavior: Alternatives module (COMPACT offers of eligible exchanges).
pageFamilies.availabilityStateBehavior: Restricted presentation per the standard; each alternative renders its own canonical state.
pageFamilies.mobileBehavior: Restriction reason + first alternative visible early; two alternatives in first-screen target.
pageFamilies.seoIndexingPosture: Indexable only when restriction-only publication is owner-authorized for that exchange x country.
prohibitedBehavior: any promo/claim/registration control for the restricted exchange

pageFamilies.name: Methodology / verification page
pageFamilies.purpose: Explain evidence, ranking, verification and update methodology.
pageFamilies.firstScreenConversionRequirement: None. No promo code is required; commercial conversion controls are never forced into methodology pages.
pageFamilies.bottomConversionBehavior: None forced; a soft directory link is permitted.
pageFamilies.availabilityStateBehavior: Explains the state system editorially; renders no commercial state.
prohibitedBehavior: commercial links blended into methodology claims

ownershipRules: Design/presentation components must not decide factual eligibility.
Market Intelligence or approved data adapters supply state.
No component may infer country eligibility from a stored code or URL.

offerRegistry: stores route and code implementation facts
prototype: must not use real affiliate navigation

rolloutOrder.status: completed and approved in design branch

prototypeRequirementsPerFamily: committed to a design branch before production migration consideration
authorityScopeNote: These authority documents approve architecture and design standards only. They do not authorize production implementation.
